decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective immediate action is to increase the thermal conductivity of the heat spreader to enhance lateral heat distribution and reduce the hotspot intensity. A moderate 20% increase is proposed, staying well within the allowed 2x step ratio.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat spreading, directly lowering the peak temperature in the chip region.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if conductivity becomes too high relative to the base plate extraction capacity
Further iterations may require adjusting base_material.conductivity or spreader geometry if this single change is insufficient
